notes: The Miracle of Istanbul — 3-0 down at half-time, six-minute comeback for the ages.
Revenge for Istanbul — Inzaghi's brace, Kaká's vintage season.
Rain-soaked Moscow final — Terry's slip, Anelka's miss, United's third European Cup.
Pep's first European crown — Puyol moved to RB, Yaya Touré improvising at CB.
Mourinho's Italian treble — Milito's brace, Eto'o tracking back as a defensive RW.
Peak tiki-taka — Mascherano repurposed as a centre-back, Wembley masterclass.
Di Matteo's miracle — Terry suspended, Bertrand handed his Chelsea debut at LM.
All-German final at Wembley — Robben's redemption goal in the 89th.
Klopp's gegenpressing BVB — Götze ruled out injured, Reus shifted central.
MSN delivers a second treble — Luis Enrique's most fearsome front three.
Madrid derby in Milan — Pepe paired with Ramos, Zidane's first European trophy.
First back-to-back CL win — Isco preferred over Bale in the latter's home city.
Three-peat sealed in Kyiv — Bale's bicycle kick off the bench, Karius's nightmare.
Klopp's first European crown at Anfield — Salah penalty inside two minutes.
Spurs' only European Cup final — Kane rushed back from injury, Lucas Moura on the bench.
Pandemic-bubble final in Lisbon — Kimmich at right-back, Coman's header sinks his boyhood club.
Courtois' masterclass — Benzema's Ballon d'Or year, three knockout comebacks before this one.
City's continental treble — Akanji in for the injured Walker, Rodri's only goal of the night.
Iconic Premier League restart after the COVID-19 shutdown.
Final-day win that sealed Milan's return to the Champions League after a seven-season absence. Kessié scored both goals from the penalty spot (43', 90'+3').
2010 World Cup semifinal. Van Bronckhorst opened the scoring with one of the greatest World Cup goals ever. Netherlands advanced to the final to face Spain.
Tadić's 69th-minute goal won it at Old Trafford. Part of Southampton's brilliant 2014-15 season under Ronald Koeman.
2022 World Cup Round of 16. Morocco became the first African nation to reach the quarterfinals since Ghana 2010. Spain missed all three penalties — Sarabia, Soler, and Busquets.
Opening weekend of 2017-18. A stunning away win at Stamford Bridge — Vokes scored twice and Ward added a third. One of the biggest upsets of that Premier League season.
Championship fixture during Villa's relegation-era rebuild. Adomah, Kodjia, and Gestede (pen, 90') scored. Grealish was Player of the Match — an early glimpse of what was to come.
A statement win during Leicester's historic title-winning season. Mahrez, Huth (x2) scored. Vardy and Kanté were unplayable. Huth scored twice from set pieces — classic Ranieri Leicester.

2018 World Cup opening match on home soil. Cheryshev came off the bench and scored twice. Golovin (9.4 rating, POTM) capped the rout in stoppage time with a free kick — one of the tournament's breakout performances.
Sergio Ramos scored an OG and then equalised with a 90'+5 pen to make it 3-2 — but Sevilla held on. Ben Yedder and Layún also scored. Asensio was POTM despite being on the losing side.
Coupe de France semifinal. Ben Yedder opened from the spot (54') and Volland sealed it (61'). Tchouaméni and Fofana in the same midfield — both would go on to Real Madrid within two years.

UCL Round of 16 second leg. Arsenal overturned a 2-1 first leg deficit with a stunning 5-0. Bendtner hat-trick (9', 25', 90'+1 pen) — one of the great individual UCL performances of the era. Nasri and Eboué also scored.
Final day of the 2016-17 season. Feghouli (27') and Ayew (72') won it for West Ham. Slaven Bilić's side finishing a turbulent first season at the London Stadium.
FA Cup fourth round upset at Anfield. Jay Rodriguez scored twice (11', 7') and Vokes added a third. Matip OG and Salah replied but wasn't enough.
Firmino (9'), Mané (40'), Wijnaldum (90'+1) won it. Screenshot partially cut off on the left — Mané and Wijnaldum inferred from match records.
FA Cup third round north London derby. Cazorla (31') and Rosický (62') scored. A young Gnabry (#44) started on the left.
Richarlison brace (38', 60'), Udogie (26'), and Son pen (85'). Son rated 9.5 — POTM. Ange Postecoglou's Spurs in full flow.
Isaac Hayden's 90'+4 winner. Classic Steve Bruce low-block. Saint-Maximin caused chaos from the right.
Jorginho (83') and Abraham (87') came from behind to win it after Aubameyang's opener. Lampard's Chelsea grinding out a result at the Emirates.
Rooney hat-trick (18', 28', 66') plus Williams (78'). Rooney's return to Everton that season — this was his best performance.
Eli Junior Kroupi (17') and Alex Scott (74') won it. Győkeres replied from the spot for Arsenal (35'). Impressive away win for Bournemouth.
Patrick Bamford hat-trick (55', 67', 74'). Leeds' first Premier League season back — Bielsa's side at their relentless best. Bamford with a 9.5 rating.
Wesley Moraes brace (14', 30'), Grealish (49'), Hourihane (61'), Douglas Luiz (83'). Grealish (c) with a dominant away performance.
One of the biggest upsets of the 2019-20 season. McLean (18'), Cantwell (28'), Pukki (50') beat the champions. Agüero and Rodri replied but it wasn't enough.
One of the most shocking Bundesliga results in recent memory. Paciência (85'), Hinteregger (61'), Abraham (49') and two others dismantled Bayern at home.
UCL Round of 16 first leg. Robben (11'), Lewandowski (53'), Thiago (56', 63'), Müller (88'). Sánchez pulled one back for Arsenal. Boateng received a red card.
Lewandowski brace (37', 85'), Kimmich (82'), Müller (76') plus two others. Lewandowski POTM with a 9.0. Kimmich starting at LB — his final season in that role before the permanent move to CM.
Özcan OG (36'), Weghorst (55'), Steffen (60') for Wolfsburg. Bailey replied for Leverkusen (24'). Solid away win for Wolfsburg.
Palacios scored twice from the spot (55', 73') — POTM 8.6. Kimmich had opened the scoring (22'). An early signal of what Xabi Alonso's Leverkusen were becoming.
Götze (62'), Guerreiro (67'), Witsel (90'), and two others. Bakalorz replied for Hannover (86'). BVB flying at the top of the table mid-season.
Beier (7'), Nmecha (40'), Brandt (66'), Gittens (77'). Brandt captaining, clean sheet. Comfortable home win.
UCL Group F. Aubameyang (43') and Schürrle (87') for BVB. Ronaldo (17') and Varane (68') for Real Madrid. Schmelzer captaining. A young Dembélé starting on the left.
UCL QF in the Lisbon bubble tournament. Dani Olmo (50') and Tyler Adams (88') won it. João Félix pen (71') for Atlético. Leipzig's run to the UCL semis — Upamecano and Angeliño shining.
Orbán (55'), Werner brace (48', 71'), Bruma (80'). Niederlechner replied for Freiburg (23'). Leipzig's second Bundesliga season — Keïta and Werner already the standouts.
Zaïre-Emery (37'), Doué (67'), Barcola (88'), Ramos (90'+2 pen). Cup tie against amateur side Espaly. Rotated squad with Tenas in goal and youth players Tape and Zague starting.
La Remontada. PSG led 4-0 from the first leg and lost 6-1 in the second. Cavani replied (62') but Suárez, Messi, Neymar (x2), and Sergi Roberto completed one of the most dramatic UCL collapses ever.

Kingsley Coman headed in the only goal (59') to give Bayern their sixth European Cup. PSG's first and only UCL final. Thiago Silva's last match for the club. Marquinhos deployed as CDM rather than CB.

Final day of the season — Lille's first Ligue 1 title in ten years, pipping PSG by one point. David (10') and Yılmaz pen (45'+1) sealed it. Maignan, Botman, Sanches all departed the following summer to major clubs.
Hernández OG (20') and Lemaréchal (45'+3) for Strasbourg; Barcola replied (46'). Georgio Doué — younger brother of PSG's Désiré — started at ST against his brother's club.
UCL Round of 16 second leg. David Luiz (66') and Thiago Silva (114') for PSG; Cahill (61') and Hazard pen (96') for Chelsea. PSG went out on away goals in devastating fashion.
Fekir brace from the spot (36', 57'), Tolisso (42', 70'), Cornet (75'). Morgan Sanson pulled one back (4'). Tolisso and Fekir were the standouts in a dominant home display.

Final day of Monaco's title-winning 2016-17 season — they finished 8 points clear of PSG. Heavy rotation with backup GK Sidy Sy starting. Fabinho (42'), Jemerson (47'), Jorge (78') scored. Mbappé (#29) started aged 18.
Volland brace (52', 65') and Fàbregas pen (64', off the bench) won it. Mbappé had scored twice for PSG (25', 37' pen). Tchouaméni and Fofana anchoring a formidable midfield.
Bodiger pen (48') and Durmaz (79') ended PSG's unbeaten start to the season. A teenage Alban Lafont (16) started in goal. One of the big upsets of the 2016-17 Ligue 1 season.
Group D. Costa Rica's shock win, Bryan Ruiz heading the only goal (44'). One of the great World Cup group-stage upsets, part of Costa Rica's run to the quarterfinals.
Group F. Peter Odemwingie's 29th-minute goal won it. Nigeria's first win of that tournament en route to the Round of 16.
Group C decider. Bony's goal (74') looked enough to send Côte d'Ivoire through, but Samaras converted a 90'+3 penalty to eliminate them and send Greece to the knockouts instead.

Round of 16. Tim Howard made a then-record 16 saves. De Bruyne (93') and Lukaku (105') won it for Belgium in extra time; substitute Julian Green pulled one back (107'). Note: position assignments for Johnson/Cameron/Dempsey are best-effort — Klinsmann made some unorthodox positional tweaks this match, worth double-checking.

Round of 16. Mexico led through Giovani dos Santos (48') until Sneijder's stunning equaliser (88') and a controversial 90'+4 Huntelaar penalty completed the comeback. Robben's dive to win the pen remains debated to this day.
Group A opener. Oribe Peralta's 61st-minute goal won it after Mexico had two earlier goals controversially disallowed for offside.

match.venue: Estádio do Maracanã, Rio de Janeiro

notes: Quarterfinal. Mats Hummels headed the only goal (12') from a corner. Germany went on to win the whole tournament.
Round of 16. David Luiz (18') and Alexis Sánchez (32') traded goals; Pinilla's shot famously struck the crossbar in the dying seconds of extra time. Brazil survived on penalties to advance.

Group A finale. Cameroon's Joel Matip ironically scored against the team that would loan him to Schalke; Neymar (x2), Fred, and Fernandinho responded. A largely chaotic and ill-disciplined Cameroon campaign came to an end.
Group C opener, played without injured captain Falcao. James Rodríguez announced himself with an assist and dominant display — the start of his Golden Boot-winning tournament.
